evals.prompt: Help me build a professional reference list for a VP of Engineering job application. I have these potential references: my direct manager at current job (great relationship but awkward to ask), my manager from 3 years ago at my last company (still in touch), a peer who I collaborated with on a major project, a direct report I mentored for 2 years, and an external partner. Guide me on who to include, in what order, and how to format the list.
evals.expected_output: A structured reference selection strategy with recommended choices, order, and a formatted reference list template
expectations: Output advises on reference selection (likely excluding current manager unless confident)
Output recommends 3-4 references appropriate for VP Engineering level
Output explains the rationale for inclusion/exclusion of each potential reference
Output provides a formatted reference list with required fields (name, title, company, relationship, contact info)
Output advises on how to brief references before they're contacted

evals.prompt: Format these reference contacts into a professional reference document for a senior marketing manager application. References: 1) John Chen, Chief Marketing Officer at Salesforce, was my skip-level manager, john.chen@salesforce.com, 415-555-0100. 2) Maria Santos, Director of Demand Gen, was my peer, maria.santos@acme.com, no phone. 3) Derek Williams, CEO at StartupXYZ, client relationship, derekw@startupxyz.io.
evals.expected_output: A professionally formatted reference list document ready for submission
expectations: Output formats all three references consistently
Output includes relationship context for each reference
Output handles the missing phone number for reference 2 gracefully
Output is formatted for professional job application submission
Output includes a header/title appropriate for the document
